mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5d returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal produces negative 20d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when either series is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained increase in the index of industrial production reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, prompting equity markets to price in near-term earnings resilience and generate positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases are followed by negative 60d returns in >60% of observations, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 3.5%, monetary policy tightens sufficiently to dampen liquidity and increase borrowing costs, triggering short-term equity sell-offs that manifest as negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real personal consumption expenditures confirms wage-driven demand expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60d returns in >58% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a declining unemployment rate indicates labor market normalization, reducing recession fears and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Declining UNRATE in recovery phases correlates with negative 20d returns in >60% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in regime-specific samples.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts drop below 2000 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where HOUST < 2000, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive value over a simple moving average benchmark.
